item.title: Andy's 'radical rewiring' and 'it's coming homes'
item.description: Andy Burnham's plans to devolve power in Britain and build more council housing lead Tuesday's papers.

item.title: Why £15 durians are being sold at half price - or given away for free
item.description: As Malaysian durians are being sold at throwaway prices, farmers worry about their livelihoods.

item.title: Sixty-six years on: The promise of DRC independence remains unfulfilled
item.description: The DRC’s independence anniversary renews debate over progress, security and state capacity.

item.title: Anxiety turns to joy as Brazil qualifies for the Final 16
item.description: Brazil fans at a fan fest on Rio de Janeiro celebrated after their team overturned Japan’s early lead and won 2-1.

item.title: Heads Up: NASA to Launch 'Daring' Telescope Rescue Mission This Week
item.description: An unprecedented mission. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: What went wrong for Scotland?
item.description: The team hoped to make history having never reached the knockout round of a World Cup - but Croatia's win over Ghana on Saturday meant their time at the tournament had to come to an end. So what happe

item.title: Families hold out hope for survivors five days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Search and rescue operations continue in Caracas, Venezuela nearly five days after the devastating double earthquakes.

item.title: How Germany were handed a World Cup humbling and what it means to Paraguay
item.description: As well as Gill's heroics, Paraguay's unlikely victory was founded on coach Alfaro's unshakeable faith in his defenders.

item.title: Morocco reach World Cup last-16 with dramatic shootout win over Netherlands
item.description: Morocco came from behind to knock the Netherlands out in the round of 32, beating them 3-2 on penalties.

item.title: The end for Nagelsmann? Germany suffer their 'next football nightmare'
item.description: Germany make a shock last-32 exit against Paraguay, losing a World Cup penalty shootout for the first time to pile pressure on boss Julian Nagelsmann.

item.title: Pupil put in isolation booth for more than half a school year, BBC learns
item.description: The pupil's experience in a Yorkshire school highlights concerns about zero-tolerance behaviour policies.

item.title: 'A joke' - how a 'terrible' VAR disallowed goal cost Germany
item.description: Alan Shearer, Jurgen Klopp, Pat Nevin and Darren Cann feel Germany were hard done by as a VAR call rules out a potential winning goal before they lose on penalties to Paraguay.

item.title: Inside Paraguay's tears of joy over their seismic World Cup shock
item.description: How Paraguay marked one of the biggest wins in their footballing history with euphoric celebrations.

item.title: Emotional Gakpo scores days after loss of unborn son
item.description: Liverpool forward Cody Gakpo scores for the Netherlands days after the loss of his unborn son, as they are knocked out on penalties by Morocco.

item.title: Meet the school with 11 pupils
item.description: Imagine your school being so remote it can even be tough for the teachers to get to. Nina went to visit the school, off the coast of Northern Ireland, where there is just one school, with two teachers

item.title: Superstar Serena: Find out about her amazing tennis story
item.description: The US superstar is a true legend of the sport and has had an amazing journey to the top based on breaking records, family bonds and incredible talent. Find out about her story here.

item.title: Australian town crier officially named world's loudest man
item.description: Joseph McGrail-Bateup's mighty yell was registered at a huge 122.4 decibels - that's around the same noise level as a jet plane taking off!

item.title: Heartbroken Iran exit World Cup amid silver lining of Mexican hospitality
item.description: In their three weeks in Tijuana, Iran's players established a reciprocal relationship of love and respect with Mexicans.

item.title: Paraguay declares holiday after beating Germany in World Cup upset
item.description: Paraguay has declared a national holiday after its national team stunned four-time champions Germany

item.title: World Cup Mexican wave fail: Tell us your celebration disasters
item.description: After one World Cup fan lost their phone during a Mexican wave, we want to hear your stories of celebration gone wrong.
